pattern: Pairing each chosen magnitude with its negation makes the sum cancel automatically, and a lone zero absorbs the leftover element when the count is odd.
hints: If you put a value and its negative into the answer, that pair contributes nothing to the total.
Build the array from symmetric pairs (x, -x) using distinct magnitudes so every entry is unique.
Emit pairs for as many slots as you can; if one slot is left over because the count is odd, fill it with zero.

edge_inputs.description: odd count needs a zero plus symmetric pairs
even count is all symmetric pairs
larger odd count - duplicates would be easy to introduce by mistake

stress.description: the maximum count to ensure uniqueness and zero-sum hold at scale
stress.note: single integer argument; reference must keep all values distinct
